summary: Phase 1 Final Restructure · Chunk B end-to-end verification. Backend: 12/13 pytest tests pass; the single failure is benign data leftover (record from a prior iter run; idempotent semantics themselves verified). Frontend: all 5 new/restyled routes (/mittari, /pelisignaalit, /voita, /peli, /) render with every required data-testid present. ProgressiveOptIn sequential funnel works end-to-end (step1 → step2 → step3 → done, never simultaneous). Client-side redirects /vihjeet → /pelisignaalit and /viikon-kortti → /pelisignaalit preserve ?ref / ?pick / ?invite query strings. /voita gated by default, switches to active hero when voita_feature_enabled=true (admin-toggled and reset to false after test). Existing pages (/menetelma, /ehdot, /tietoa-meista, /striimaajat, /back-office, /uutiset) still load with no regression. No app-level console errors.

minor.issue: test_idempotent_resubmit_does_not_duplicate fails on repeat runs because the seeded email 'idempotency-test@putkihq.example' was inserted in an earlier iteration and persists in optin_consents. The endpoint itself works correctly (resubmit returns new_record=false). Fix is test-side: randomise the email per run or pre-delete the row.

issues: The large serif state-name (e.g. ROLLING, 700px wide × 119px tall, x=818) sits to the right of the dial and visually overlays the right-hand info column on viewport widths >= 1920. No DOM overlap detected (state-name x=818 starts after dial-slot ends at 762), but the composite/signals labels on the dial card sit underneath the giant serif glyphs visually. Consider z-index isolation or a smaller serif size on narrow desktops.

action_items: (Minor, optional) Make test_idempotent_resubmit_does_not_duplicate use a random email per run so it stays green across iterations.
(Minor, optional) Tighten the /mittari hero layout so the giant serif state-name does not visually crowd the dial labels at >=1920px.
critical_code_review_comments: ProgressiveOptIn.jsx fires the telegram POST inside joinTelegram() but swallows all errors with `catch {}` — failure to record consent is silent. Consider surfacing at least a console.warn so analytics gaps are debuggable.

RedirectWithQuery uses React Router <Navigate replace> — that is a client-side redirect, NOT an HTTP 301. Search engines crawling the legacy URLs will still hit the React shell first. If SEO equivalence matters, add a 301 at the proxy/CDN layer as well.

ProgressiveOptIn re-fetches /api/settings/public on every mount to resolve telegramUrl. Multiple surfaces on the same page (homepage, mittari, etc.) will each issue a request. Consider lifting that into a shared context or memoised hook.
test_credentials: BACK_OFFICE_TOKEN=putki-hq-admin (read from /app/memory/test_credentials.md not required — provided in problem statement).
seed_data_creation: Created one unique email opt-in record per UI run (chunkb-ui-<ts>@example.com) on /mittari surface. Toggled voita_feature_enabled true→false; restored to false at end. No destructive data changes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Chunk B is shipped. If you run the backend suite, expect the idempotency test to fail until either the email is randomised or the seeded row is purged. All ProgressiveOptIn testid prefixes follow the pattern '{surface}-optin-component-stepN'. Voita active state requires PUT /api/admin/settings {voita_feature_enabled:true} and MUST be reset to false afterwards (default gated state is intentional, per agent-to-agent note).
rca of the issue: Backend idempotency assertion failure root cause: optin_consents collection retains records across pytest iterations (correct for an idempotency feature, but breaks the assertion `first_new is True`). The runtime endpoint is functioning exactly as specified — duplicate (channel, surface, identifier) returns new_record=false. Mitigation: parameterise the email with a uuid in the test, or add a teardown fixture that deletes TEST_/idempotency-* documents.
